title: Fleet Deployment Database (Beta)
content:
# Fleet Deployment Database (Beta)

## 1) Client Issue Description
Need team feedback/testing to validate new end-to-end deployment workflow database intended to replace Excel/SharePoint templates.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
SMEs to test mobile workflow (vehicle creation, barcode scan, data upload) and suggest improvements; confirm how installer performance/expectation metrics should be captured.

## 3) Root Cause
Under investigation.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Beta access currently limited (Bobby & Alex). Joe plans outreach over coming months; public release target ~6 months.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
Joe to reach out to testers; Team to provide feedback/suggestions; Joe to integrate installer performance data & other Spreadsheet information

## References / Attachments
No direct link included in the source board.

## Source Notes
- Swarm board owner / volunteers: Joe (owner), Alex, Bobby, Jordan, Fleet Masters team
- Decision needed: N
- Due: 2026-03-31

title: Installer electrical testing standards (multimeter vs power probe/test light)
content:
# Installer electrical testing standards (multimeter vs power probe/test light)

## 1) Client Issue Description
Inconsistent field testing practices; power probes/test lights can cause damage or unsafe diagnostics.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Alignment/enforcement with vendors (ProLogic/Insight) and guidance on acceptable 'computer-safe' test lights vs multimeters.

## 3) Root Cause
Root cause was identified through team alignment and process review.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Team agreed: enforce multimeter use; report misuse to Alex.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
All: enforce multimeters; report power probe use to Alex; reinforce in install standards/KB

## References / Attachments
No direct link included in the source board.

## Source Notes
- Swarm board owner / volunteers: Alex, Joe, Grant
- Decision needed: Y
- Due: 2026-02-25

title: Firmware update timeout/false-fail bug (Fleet system)
content:
# Firmware update timeout/false-fail bug (Fleet system)

## 1) Client Issue Description
Firmware update appears to hang/fail due to session timeout/log-in conflicts; may require second attempt though update completed.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Repro steps + confirm workaround (monitor via Evidence.com during update); get JIRA updated/new ticket with details.

## 3) Root Cause
Known issue; exact software or workflow cause still being validated.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Known issue discussed; Austin workaround shared

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
Bobby reproduce + document + submit/add details to existing/new JIRA; share repro in Fleet Masters Slack

## References / Attachments

title: Adhesive delamination tracking (Tau glass 3/4/5/7-in-1)
content:
# Adhesive delamination tracking (Tau glass 3/4/5/7-in-1)

## 1) Client Issue Description
Need consistent reporting of adhesive falling off/delaminating so Product team can monitor via Salesforce dashboard.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Confirm exact reporting steps + ensure everyone follows process; capture photos/agency details.

## 3) Root Cause
Root cause was identified through team alignment and process review.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Process defined: email Fleeteployments@axon.com with 'adhesive' in subject + create Salesforce case.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
All: email + case whenever reported; include details/photos; continue to track.

## References / Attachments
Link / reference: Fleeteployments@axon.com

## Source Notes
- Swarm board owner / volunteers: All team members, Product
- Decision needed: N
- Due: 2026-12-31

title: Flip & Rip removal guidelines / airbag wiring risk
content:
# Flip & Rip removal guidelines / airbag wiring risk

## 1) Client Issue Description
Improper removal can leave old wiring under airbags; creates safety/liability risk and refresh delays.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Ensure guidelines are followed and exceptions documented; determine where to log/document cases and notify POC.

## 3) Root Cause
Under investigation.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Alex released guidelines; team to document any removals where light bar wiring is under airbags and notify POC.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
All: document in workbook, notify POC, share documentation to protect against claims; reinforce in training/KB

## References / Attachments
No direct link included in the source board.

## Source Notes
- Swarm board owner / volunteers: Alex, Installers, Fleet Masters
- Decision needed: N
- Due: 2026-02-28

title: Pre-removal video offload from hubs (refreshes)
content:
# Pre-removal video offload from hubs (refreshes)

## 1) Client Issue Description
Risk of data loss if old Fleet 3 hardware removed before videos are offloaded.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Standardize pre-removal checklist + reminders to installers/partners.

## 3) Root Cause
Under investigation.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Team reminder: ensure all videos offloaded before removal; Bobby cited recent TPL handoff needing extra work.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
All: add to removal checklist and enforce; call out noncompliance in QC notes.

title: Reactivation sensitivity setting left at default delay
content:
# Reactivation sensitivity setting left at default delay

## 1) Client Issue Description
Immediate activation often desired by agencies, but setting left deselected (30-60 Second delay) causes operational delays.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Confirm default standard + exceptions; embed into QC / config checklist.

## 3) Root Cause
Under investigation.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Joe notes most agencies want immediate activation; Alex mentioned exceptions when directed by POC.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
All: Have the conversation with the POC regarding this feature to see if they actually would indeed want this disabled

title: Agency APN/SIM configuration confusion (wrong APN) + Evidence.com profile setup
content:
# Agency APN/SIM configuration confusion (wrong APN) + Evidence.com profile setup

## 1) Client Issue Description
Agencies may use incorrect APN/SIM config and misunderstand Evidence.com profile/ownership, blocking dashboard access and adoption.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Create KB article documenting Austin case, best practices, and potential solution (IP range settings/traffic steering) to reduce APN dependence.

## 3) Root Cause
Likely caused by a known configuration, workflow, or product behavior identified during team review.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Austin resolved wrong APN scenario; discussed Evidence.com individual profiles; Alex proposed IP range/traffic steering approach.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
Bobby: write KB article from transcript + share in Fleet KB/Slack; Austin/Alex explore client solution options.

## References / Attachments
No direct link included in the source board.

title: Travel contingencies + expense reporting cadence
content:
# Travel contingencies + expense reporting cadence

## 1) Client Issue Description
Weather can cause flight cancellations/delays; need standard comms and on-time expense submissions.

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Clarify escalation path and required comms when travel delays occur; standardize expense entry format/purpose code.

## 3) Root Cause
Under investigation.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Alex: communicate delays with him + agency; use Fleet Masters channel before wider escalations; expenses due end of every Monday w/ correct CKO code.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
All: message Alex+agency if delayed; post issues in Fleet Masters channel; submit expenses by Monday (tag agency + correct purpose code).

title: Proper Grounding and how to test if it is a good ground
content:
# Proper Grounding and how to test if it is a good ground

## 1) Client Issue Description
If a ground is installed with having high resistance it can cause numorous issues with Fleet3

## 2) Observed Symptoms
Clarified/asked team to ensure everyone knows how to properly test if it’s a good ground (Using Multimeter set it to ohms and put one lead on your ground stud and the other lead on the chassis of the vehicle), you should have less then .03 ohms reading to be a proper ground if the number is higher then 1 then do not use this as your ground for Fleet3.

## 3) Root Cause
Under investigation.

## 4) Resolution Performed
Clarified/asked team to ensure everyone knows how to properly test if it’s a good ground (Using Multimeter set it to ohms and put one lead on your ground stud and the other lead on the chassis of the vehicle), you should have less then .03 ohms reading to be a proper ground if the number is higher then 1 then do not use this as your ground for Fleet3.

## 5) Prevention / Education Provided
Create KB Article

## References / Attachments
No direct link included in the source board.

- Decision needed: N
